learnings.content: cortex v3 post_tool_use hook over-fires on Bash/Write/Edit because the skipworthy() filter only excludes Read/Glob/Grep/TodoWrite/Task*. Every routine command (mkdir, ls, file write) produces a discovery-tagging directive prepended to context, costing tokens. Fix: expand skipworthy or invert the filter (only fire on a small allowlist of high-signal tools like WebFetch).
